messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] GCC 5.3.0 - sd(NA) = NaN?
messages.body_plain:
Hello.

As an experiment, I tried compiling R with the mingw64 5.3.0 (posix-seh)
build. R compiles smoothly, but make check fails pretty early as sd(NA)
returns NaN instead of NA, which is a fatal error in the tests.I have no
idea what may cause this, but thought it may be of interest to this group.
I'd appreciate it if someone who understands why that happens would post a
brief explanation.

Thanks,

Avi

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] GCC 5.3.0 - sd(NA) = NaN?
messages.body_plain:
I found something similar a while back:
http://r.789695.n4.nabble.com/Optimization-bug-when-byte-compiling-with-gcc-5-2-0-on-windows-td4712220.html

IIRC the issue was diagnosed by Luke that there are a few workarounds
that currently use: #if __GNUC__ <= 4. However when changing that to
5, all kind of other problems appeared.

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Suggested change to RHOME/src/gnuwin32/fixed/etc/Makeconf for R-3.3.0
messages.subject_clean: Suggested change to RHOME/src/gnuwin32/fixed/etc/Makeconf for R-3.3.0
messages.body_plain:
As R-3.3.0 will use gcc 4.9.3 with full support for C11, perhaps
CXX1XSTD = -std=c++0x should be replaced by CXX1XSTD = -std=c++11
there?

Most of the time, packages build from .R/Makevars, but sometimes, and
I don't know why, they default to the fixed/etc.

Thank you,

Avi

messages.subject: [R-SIG-Win] Suggested change to RHOME/src/gnuwin32/fixed/etc/Makeconf for R-3.3.0
messages.subject_clean: Suggested change to RHOME/src/gnuwin32/fixed/etc/Makeconf for R-3.3.0
messages.body_plain:
On 30/03/2016 6:10 PM, Avraham Adler wrote:
> As R-3.3.0 will use gcc 4.9.3 with full support for C11, perhaps
> CXX1XSTD = -std=c++0x should be replaced by CXX1XSTD = -std=c++11
> there?
>
> Most of the time, packages build from .R/Makevars, but sometimes, and
> I don't know why, they default to the fixed/etc.

I think it's too late for a change like that.  I don't know if there are 
any c++0x features which are illegal in c++11, but if so, the package 
authors would be left in the lurch.

Duncan Murdoch
